column1: question
Define deep learning. How is it different from traditional machine learning?
What is a convolutional neural network? List its main layers.
Define activation function. Give three examples used in deep learning.
What is batch normalization? Why is it used?
Define dropout. How does it prevent overfitting?
What is a recurrent neural network? What problem does it solve?
Define LSTM. What problem of vanilla RNN does it address?
What is transfer learning? Give two examples of pre-trained models.
Define an autoencoder. List its components.
What is a GAN? Define generator and discriminator.
Explain the backpropagation algorithm through time (BPTT). How does it suffer from vanishing gradients?
Describe the architecture of ResNet. How do skip connections solve the vanishing gradient problem?
Explain the attention mechanism in neural networks. How does self-attention work in the Transformer architecture?
Describe the YOLO object detection algorithm. How does it achieve real-time detection?
Explain the working of a Variational Autoencoder (VAE). How does it differ from a standard autoencoder?
Compare CNN and Vision Transformer (ViT) for image classification. Analyze their data requirements and inductive biases.
Explain the Transformer architecture in detail. How do multi-head attention and positional encoding work together?
Describe the training process of GANs. What are mode collapse and training instability? How can they be addressed?
Explain the concept of neural architecture search (NAS). How does it automate the design of optimal network architectures?
Describe the BERT pre-training objectives (MLM and NSP). How is fine-tuning performed for downstream tasks?
Design a deep learning architecture for detecting plant diseases from leaf images with limited labeled data.
Critically evaluate the interpretability of deep neural networks. Propose explainability techniques for a medical imaging application.
Analyze the computational and environmental cost of training large language models. Propose efficiency improvements.
Design a federated learning system for training a deep learning model on sensitive healthcare data across hospitals.
Formulate a curriculum learning strategy for training a model on a dataset with varying difficulty samples.
Critically compare knowledge distillation and model pruning for deploying deep learning models on edge devices.
Propose a neural network architecture for multi-modal learning combining image and text inputs. Justify design choices.
Evaluate the use of adversarial training for improving robustness of deep learning models. Discuss trade-offs.
Design a self-supervised learning framework for learning visual representations without labeled data.
Critically analyze the scaling laws for large language models. What do they imply for future AI development?
What is semantic segmentation? How does the U-Net architecture achieve it?
Explain the concept of word embeddings. How does Word2Vec learn them using skip-gram and CBOW?
What is reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF)? How is it used to align language models?
Explain the concept of neural style transfer. How does it use CNN feature maps?
What is a capsule network? How does it improve upon CNNs for pose estimation?
Explain the concept of contrastive learning. How does SimCLR use it for self-supervised representation learning?
What is quantization in deep learning? How does it reduce model size and inference time?
Explain the concept of depthwise separable convolutions. How does MobileNet use them for efficiency?
What is the vanishing gradient problem? How do LSTM and ResNet architectures address it?
Explain the concept of data augmentation in deep learning. Give five techniques used for image classification.
